title: Well-Known Ports
description:
## Default Ports

Many protocols have **well-known (default) port numbers**: 22 for SSH, 80 for HTTP, 443 for HTTPS, 53 for DNS, and 25 for SMTP (mail).

A web service is serving traffic over **HTTPS** with no custom port configured. Which port is it listening on by default?
explanation: **HTTPS** (HTTP over TLS) uses port **443** by default. Port **80** (a) is plain **HTTP**, port **22** (b) is **SSH** for secure remote login/transfer, and port **53** (d) is **DNS** name resolution. For completeness, port **25** is **SMTP** for mail transfer. Knowing these defaults lets you read firewall rules and connection strings at a glance — `https://example.com` is implicitly `:443`, just as `http://example.com` is implicitly `:80`.

hints.title: HTTP vs HTTPS ports
hints.body: Plain HTTP and encrypted HTTPS sit on two adjacent-in-memory but different ports: one is 80, the other is 443.
